data.title: General Dynamics downgraded at Jefferies on profit worries
data.description:
• General Dynamics (NYSE:GD) on Thursday was downgraded to Hold from a previous investment rating of Buy among analysts at financial-services firm Jefferies. They said the aerospace and defense company’s profits will be limited as long as deliveries of Gulfstream jets are delayed, and that its shipbuilding business faces labor and supply-chain constraints.
  • “We assume 143 Gulfstream deliveries in 2024 and 160 in 2025 with 2024 below guidance on seven fewer G700s at 35 versus guidance of 42,” Sheila Kahyaoglu, analyst at Jefferies, said in a December 12 report.
  • Jefferies lowered its price target on General Dynamics (GD) to $300 a share from $345 a share previously, based on an estimated multiple of 17 times earnings.

data.title: A Deep Dive Into the Nasdaq's Road to 20,000
data.description:
Wall Street's tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) index on Wednesday made history by crossing and closing above 20,000 points for the first time ever.

The venerable gauge was launched back on February 5, 1971. It includes all domestic and international stocks listed on the Nasdaq stock market, tracking more than 3,000 companies using a straightforward market capitalization weighting scheme.

Due to its use of computerized trading technology, the Nasdaq Composite became a favorite place for the companies that emerged in the internet boom of the 1990s. The index ultimately became the first exchange to accommodate Silicon Valley's tech firms, cementing its image as a tech-heavy average in the future.

It took the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) 12,386 trading days - or a little over 49 years - since its launch to hit 10,000 points. It then took the Nasdaq (COMP:IND) just 1,124 trading days - or about four and a half years - to hit 20,000 points, underscoring the powerful nature of advancement in technology and its dominant role in our lives today.

See below for a chart and an accompanying breakdown of some significant dates in the Nasdaq Composite's history:

  • February 5, 1971: The Nasdaq's very first close at 100 points.
  • April 12, 1991: The index settled above 500 points for the first time, following steady gains through the 1980s.
  • July 17, 1995: The gauge took out the 1,000 points closing level, at a time when the popularity of computers and web browsers were exploding, leading the U.S. into the information age.
  • March 10, 2000: The Nasdaq peaked at this level a day after taking out the 5,000 points mark for the first time. The dot-com bubble burst after this, leading to a significant crash that would send the index spiraling over the next two years.
  • October 10, 2002: The index bottomed at this level after the dot-com crash, then began to recover.
  • October 31, 2007: This was the peak of the Nasdaq's recovery from the dot-com crash, as the great financial crisis unfolded.
  • March 9, 2009: The gauge bottomed at this level during the financial crisis.
  • April 23, 2015: After just over fifteen long years, the Nasdaq Composite reclaimed its closing level from 2000.
  • June 10, 2020: The index closed above 10,000 points for the first time ever, despite the COVID-19 pandemic.
  • August 24, 2021: The average concluded above 15,000 points for the first time ever.
  • November 7, 2024: In the aftermath of former U.S. President Donald Trump's election victory, the "Trump trade" that gripped markets propelled the Nasdaq past 19,000 points for the first time ever.
  • December 11, 2024: Nearly 54 years after its launch, the index hits 20,000.

Looking back at the Nasdaq's history, a major event was in 2015 when the index once again reached the level it had claimed in 2000. The gauge's last big push to the 20,000 milestone took place from 2018 to 2024, a time period in which it climbed 13,000 points.

Notably, the Nasdaq's final rise from 19,000 to 20,000 was one of its third-shortest gap between 1,000-point milestones since 2018. See the chart below:

With the advent of artificial intelligence and the demand for it showing no signs of stopping, the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) is in a good position for further gains.

Here are some exchange-traded funds of interest tied to the Nasdaq and tech: (QQQ), (QLD), (TQQQ), (QID), (SQQQ), (VGT), (XLK), (IYW), (FTEC), (IXN), and (RSPT).

  • U.S. ETFs notch new record as inflows cross $1T this year

data.title: Imperial Oil sees FY25 capital expenditures C$1.9B-C$2.1B
data.description:
• Imperial Oil (NYSE:IMO) remains focused on maximizing the value of its existing assets and progressing advantaged high-value growth opportunities while delivering industry-leading returns to shareholders.
  • Capital and exploration expenditures are forecasted to range between C$1.9 to C$2.1B.
  • In the Upstream, production is forecasted to grow to between 433,000 and 456,000 gross oil equivalent barrels/day. In the Downstream, throughput is forecasted to be between 405,000 and 415,000 barrels/day with capacity utilization between 94% and 96%.
  • The company is planning turnarounds at each of its refineries in 2025, with lower expected impacts to throughput and costs compared to 2024.
  • "Our 2025 plan builds on our momentum and positions the company to achieve even stronger operating performance with higher volumes and lower unit cash costs at Kearl and Cold Lake." said Brad Corson, chairman, president and CEO.

data.title: Kodiak Gas Services announces pricing of 5.5M shares of common stock offering by selling shareholders
data.description:
• Kodiak Gas Services (NYSE:KGS) on Thursday announced the pricing of an underwritten offering of 5.5M shares of its common stock, resulting in total gross proceeds of $232.93 million.
  • Kodiak will not sell any shares of its common stock in the offering and will not receive any proceeds from the sale of the shares of its common stock being offered by the selling stockholder.
  • The offering is expected to close on December 13, 2024, subject to customary closing conditions.
  • KGS is -3.58291% to $41.98.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: YouTube TV's monthly price goes up by $10 as content costs rise
data.description:
Google (NASDAQ:GOOGL) (NASDAQ:GOOG) has raised the subscription price for YouTube TV by $10 to $82.99 per month, due to rising costs for content, the company said in a blog post Thursday.

The company said the updated price will continue to include 100+ channels, unlimited DVR storage, up to 6 accounts per household, and 3 concurrent streams; no additional fees would be required for broadcast, HD, set-top box, or DVR.

The new price for YouTube TV matches with Hulu, which provides Live TV, Disney+, and ESPN+ with ads for the same price.

Changes in the prices will be effective from today, the company said.

data.title: Avalanche raises $250M in Galaxy Digital-led token sale; AVAX jumps
data.description:
Avalanche, a layer-1 blockchain undergoing a major overhaul, said Thursday it has raised $250M in a locked-token sale led by Michael Novogratz's Galaxy Digital (OTCPK:BRPHF), Dragonfly, and ParaFi Capital.

The native token of Avalanche, AVAX (AVAX-USD), jumped 12% at press time amid the announcement. It's up some 26% this year vs. ether's (ETH-USD) 68% climb.

The capital raise comes after the project's rollout of Avalanche9000 testnet, an upgrade that sought to lower transaction and development costs. The objective with the software upgrade is to make it easier for developers to build apps on Avalanche.

More than 40 investment firms played a role in the deal, Avalanche said in a statement.

data.title: Google, Samsung show off mixed reality headset dev kit to take on Apple, Meta
data.description:
Google (NASDAQ:GOOG) (GOOGL) and Samsung (OTCPK:SSNLF) jointly unveiled a mixed reality headset developer kit on Thursday that the pair hope will usher in further competition for Apple's (NASDAQ:AAPL) and Meta Platforms' (NASDAQ:META) competing devices.

The headset uses the Android XR (extended reality) operating system, which was showcased and unveiled at a developer event in New York. The new headset is part of Samsung's Project Moohan (Korean for "infinite") initiative, intended to further diversify its consumer electronics.

Google added that Sony (SONY) and other companies will use the Android XR operating system for their respective headsets.

Pricing of the new Google-Samsung headset is yet to be determined, but the duo are mindful that Apple's Vision Pro has been slow to take off, with its $3,499 price tag. Samsung told Bloomberg it is "doing a lot of research on what price point customers will feel comfortable paying."

Conversely, Meta's Quest devices start at $299, though they are less powerful than the Vision Pro.

Previous reports have indicated that Apple is likely to sell less than 500,000 Vision Pro units this year and may have "sharply" reduced production of the mixed reality headset since the summer.

The first products based on the Project Moohan (Korean for "infinite") platform will launch early next year, Google and Samsung said.

In addition, the two companies teased that smart glasses will be a part of the project, though they did not offer a time frame for the product.

data.title: Sectra AB (publ) reports Q2 results
data.description:
• Sectra AB (publ) press release (OTCPK:STKTF): Q2 profit of SEK 87.8 million.
  • Net sales of SEK 753.5 million.
  • The company's order bookings for Q2 surpassed SEK 4 billion.

data.title: Trevi stock rockets on Haduvio study update
data.description:
Trevi Therapeutics (NASDAQ:TRVI) shares shot up as much as 81% Thursday after the company said it won’t need to change the sample size for an ongoing Phase 2b study of its drug Haduvio for chronic cough.

The company said the independent data statistician for the clinical trial has reaffirmed the study’s current sample size of N=160. The analysis was conducted on the highest dose in the study, 108mg twice daily.

The study, called CORAL, is evaluating three dose levels of Haduvio, also known as oral nalbuphine ER, for the treatment of chronic cough in patients with idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis. Trevi (NASDAQ:TRVI) said the study has reached 75% enrollment, with topline results expected in the first half of 2025, according to a statement.

Trevi (TRVI) added that it was pleased by the outcome of the sample size re-estimation analysis as it supports continued execution of the trial as originally planned and confirms key powering assumptions in the trial’s design.

Earlier this month, Trevi (TRVI) shares tumbled post-market after the company reported topline results from a human abuse potential study of Haduvio.

data.title: WFE 2025 outlook cut lower due to China, lower DRAM demand: Jefferies
data.description:
Lam Research (NASDAQ:LRCX) and KLA (NASDAQ:KLAC) price targets were reduced by Jefferies due to a combination of weaker wafer fab equipment from China and a slowdown in memory demand.

"We are cutting our CY25 WFE outlook further to reflect a lower estimate for China (now 20% of WFE from 30%+) and less strength in DRAM," said Jefferies analysts, led by Blayne Curtis, in a note. "From our perspective, the only real strength is from TSMC (NYSE:TSM) at the leading edge, but there are questions around Foundry strategy at both Samsung (OTCPK:SSNLF) and Intel (NASDAQ:INTC)."

Jefferies reduced its price targets on Lam Research to $90 from $95 and KLA to $770 from $875, while retaining a Buy rating on both stocks. However, the investment firm slightly increased its price target on Applied Materials (NASDAQ:AMAT) to $185 from $180 while maintaining a Hold rating.

"While we do still see some longer-term upside in the group and recognize this is a group that investors will want to revisit, we struggle with the setup into January earnings, as estimates still need to come down," Curtis added.

Lam Research looks to have the most near-term upside of this group due to stronger NAND demand.

Jefferies now expects 5% WFE growth in calendar year 2024, zero growth in 2025 and 6% growth in 2026.
